body_tmpl: Hi, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I own the property at {ADDRESS}. I want to widen my driveway by about six feet on the north side to fit a third vehicle. Do I need a permit for that, and if so what documents do I need to submit? Also, is there a fee involved? Thanks for any help you can provide.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, yes, a driveway expansion permit is required in Bellwood. You'll need to submit a site plan showing the proposed dimensions, the distance from the property line, and the material (concrete or asphalt). The fee is {AMOUNT}. You can apply online at our portal or drop off at Village Hall. Let me know if you have questions.

body_tmpl: I'm {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. I'd like to install a six-foot privacy fence along my rear and side property lines. My neighbor at {NUM} {CROSS} is on board. I looked at your website but couldn't find the fence permit form. Can you email it to me, or tell me where to pick it up? I'd also like to know the turnaround time once I submit.

{FIRST} {LAST} here, {ADDRESS}. I want to build a 20x24 detached garage in my backyard. I have drawings from my contractor. What zoning setbacks apply for a detached structure in an R-1 zone? And what is the permit fee schedule — is it based on square footage or a flat rate? I'd like to get started before {DATE} if possible.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST} — in R-1 the rear setback for an accessory structure is five feet and side is three feet. Fee is based on valuation: submit your contractor's cost estimate and we calculate from there. Bring drawings plus the estimate to our counter or upload via the portal. Standard review is {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Deck permit — how long does approval take?
body_tmpl: Good morning. I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, homeowner at {ADDRESS}. My contractor says he needs a permit before starting our rear deck (16x20, pressure-treated lumber, attached to house). We submitted our application on {DATE}. I haven't heard anything back and it's been {DAYS} days. Can you check on the status? Permit case number is {CASE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, I pulled up case {CASE}. We're waiting on a revised site plan showing the ledger attachment detail. Once we receive that, review should wrap up within two to three business days. Please have your contractor resubmit the updated drawing.

body_tmpl: Hi there — I had my water heater replaced yesterday by a licensed plumber. He said Bellwood requires a permit even for a like-for-like replacement. We forgot to pull the permit beforehand. Is there a way to get a retroactive permit or after-the-fact inspection? The plumber's license number is on file with the state. My address is {ADDRESS}. Please let me know what the process is.

My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. I'm upgrading from a 100-amp to a 200-amp service panel. My electrician says she'll pull the permit but I want to confirm the Village requires one and understand what inspections are involved. Do we need a rough-in inspection, a final, or both? Also, does the inspector need access to the inside of the house?
reply_tmpl: Yes, an electrical permit is required for service upgrades. Inspections: rough-in (before panel cover goes on) and final. Inspector will need access inside to verify the panel and at least a sample of circuits. Your electrician can schedule online or call {PHONE}.

body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. I received a stop-work order posted on my door at {ADDRESS} this morning. My contractor assured me all permits were in order. Case {CASE}. I need to understand exactly what the violation is so I can get it resolved immediately — my crew is standing by and losing time. Can someone call me at {PHONE} or respond by email today?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — the stop-work was issued because the foundation inspection was not scheduled before pouring. Please have your contractor call {PHONE} to schedule a footing inspection. Once that passes, the hold will be released same day.

body_tmpl: We just finished a basement finishing project at {ADDRESS} and need the certificate of occupancy before we can rent it out. Final inspection passed on {DATE} per case {CASE}. How long does it take to receive the CO, and is it mailed or do we pick it up at Village Hall? Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Congratulations on the final inspection, {FIRST}. The CO is typically issued within {DAYS} business days after final approval. You can pick it up at the Building Department counter or request we mail it. We'll send an email when it's ready.

body_tmpl: Hello — I submitted a permit application last week (case {CASE}, {ADDRESS}) and got an approval email but I'm not sure how to pay the {AMOUNT} fee. The online portal shows a balance due but the payment link doesn't seem to work. Can I pay by credit card over the phone or come in to Village Hall? Hours would be helpful too.

Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My deck framing is complete and I need to schedule the framing inspection before we install the decking boards. Permit case {CASE}. What's the earliest available slot? Mornings work best for me. The contractor will also be on site.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — we have a morning slot on {DATE} at {TIME}. Does that work? Inspector will need access to the ledger connection, joists, and beam bearing points. Please confirm and I'll lock it in.

body_tmpl: I have an active permit for a garage addition at {ADDRESS} (case {CASE}) that expires on {DATE}. Due to contractor delays I won't be finished in time. What is the process to extend? Is there an additional fee? I've already passed the rough framing inspection, so we're more than halfway done.
reply_tmpl: Extensions are available for up to six months. Submit a written request referencing case {CASE} along with the {AMOUNT} extension fee. Since rough framing has passed, your extension should be straightforward. Drop off at the counter or email the request and we'll send a payment link.

subject_tmpl: Do I need a permit to replace my roof?
body_tmpl: {FIRST} here at {ADDRESS}. Getting quotes to tear off and replace my asphalt shingle roof. Two contractors say I need a village permit; one says I don't. Can you clarify? Also, does the inspector need to see the sheathing before new shingles go on, or is a final inspection enough? Just trying to plan.
reply_tmpl: A roofing permit is required in Bellwood for full tear-offs. We do a final inspection after completion — no mid-project inspection unless decking replacement is involved. Fee is {AMOUNT}. Your contractor can apply on your behalf.

body_tmpl: Good afternoon. I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. We want to install an above-ground pool, approximately 18 feet in diameter. I understand there may be permit and fencing requirements. Can you tell me what's needed — permit application, site plan, fence height, distance from property line? We'd like to have it set up before summer.

subject_tmpl: HVAC replacement permit — does homeowner need one?
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {NAME}, homeowner at {ADDRESS}. I'm replacing my central A/C and furnace. My HVAC company said their license covers the permit pull, but I want to verify with the Village directly. Is a permit required for a furnace and A/C replacement on existing equipment in a single-family home? What's the fee? Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Yes, a mechanical permit is required for HVAC replacements. Your contractor should apply — fee is {AMOUNT}. One inspection is required after installation, before the equipment is enclosed. If you don't hear from your contractor within a few days, feel free to check status at Village Hall.

body_tmpl: Hi — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. We're replacing all the vinyl siding on our house. The contractor is ready to start on {DATE}. Do we need a permit? If so, can it be pulled same-day or does it require review time? I want to make sure we're legal before they start tearing the old stuff off.

{FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I'm getting rooftop solar installed. The installer (out of state) says Illinois has a standard permit process but wants me to confirm Bellwood's requirements. Specifically: do you require engineered drawings, and is there a special electrical inspection? The system is 8 kW, grid-tied. Permit fee?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — Bellwood requires a building permit and electrical permit for solar. You'll need: manufacturer specs, structural letter from engineer if roof age is over 15 years, and single-line electrical diagram. Fees total {AMOUNT}. Final inspection by our electrical inspector is required before net-metering application.

body_tmpl: Hi there. My electrical rough-in inspection failed yesterday (case {CASE}, {ADDRESS}). The inspector left a correction notice but I'm having trouble reading the handwriting on item 3. Can you email me a typed copy of the corrections? I want to make sure I fix everything correctly before scheduling the re-inspection.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST} — I'll have the inspector type up the correction items and email them to you today. Typically re-inspections are scheduled within {DAYS} business days once you notify us the corrections are complete. No additional fee for the first re-inspection.

body_tmpl: Good morning — I'm {FIRST} at {ADDRESS}. I want to put up a prefab metal shed in my backyard, about 10x12. My neighbor said sheds under 100 square feet don't need a permit. Is that accurate for Bellwood? If a permit is required, what's the process and fee?

{NAME} at {ADDRESS} here. We want to add a half-bath in our unfinished basement. Plumber says it requires a plumbing permit; I assume we'd also need a building permit. Do both get applied for at the same time through one application? And will an ejector pump installation require a separate inspection? Thank you.
reply_tmpl: You can submit both under one application — we'll issue separate building and plumbing permit numbers. Ejector pump is included in the plumbing inspection scope. A framing inspection may also be needed if you're adding walls. Fee depends on scope: estimate {AMOUNT} combined. Submit plans showing existing drain locations and proposed layout.

body_tmpl: Hi, this is {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I'm replacing all six double-hung windows on my house with energy-efficient ones, same size, no structural changes. Does Bellwood require a permit for like-for-like window replacements? The contractor says it depends on the municipality. If yes, can he pull it online?

Hello — I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I own {ADDRESS}. We're doing a full kitchen gut remodel: new cabinets, counters, moving a gas line, adding a circuit for the range. I know gas and electrical need permits. Do I also need a general building permit, or do the trade permits cover it? I'd like to understand total fees upfront.
reply_tmpl: For a kitchen remodel with gas and electrical work you'll need: mechanical/plumbing permit for the gas line and a separate electrical permit for the circuit. If you're moving any walls a building permit is also required. Fees: electrical {AMOUNT}, plumbing/gas add {AMOUNT}. No structural building permit needed if walls stay.

body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST}. I recently purchased {ADDRESS} and I'm trying to find out what permits have been pulled on this property over the past ten years. Are older permit records available to the public? Who should I contact, and is there a fee for the search? I want to make sure everything was done legally by the previous owners.

I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. I hired a contractor to remodel my bathroom. The job is nearly done and I just found out he never pulled a permit. I'm worried about selling the house later. What do I do now — can I get a retroactive permit or an as-built inspection? I don't want to be penalized for something my contractor did.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — this situation is not uncommon. You can apply for an after-the-fact permit; the inspector will need access to verify the work. If any work is concealed, some opening may be required. The permit fee is standard plus a {AMOUNT} late fee. Contact our office to get started and we'll walk you through it.

body_tmpl: {NAME} here at {ADDRESS}. My wife and I want to build a 12x16 freestanding pergola in our backyard. No roof, just a lattice top. Does that require a permit in Bellwood? And are there height restrictions or setback requirements for a freestanding structure like that?

subject_tmpl: Permit approved — when can work start?
body_tmpl: Hi — I just got an email that my driveway permit (case {CASE}, {ADDRESS}) has been approved. Does my contractor need the actual permit card on site before starting, or is the email approval sufficient? Also, does the inspector need to see the subbase before we pour, or is a final inspection after pouring enough?
reply_tmpl: Congrats, {FIRST}! Your contractor should print the permit approval and keep it on site. For concrete driveways we do require a subbase inspection before pouring — please schedule that at least {DAYS} business days in advance. Call {PHONE} or use the online portal to book.

body_tmpl: Hello, I'm the manager of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. We want to replace our existing wall sign with a new LED backlit sign, same location, larger by about 20%. I understand a sign permit is required. What are the size limits for commercial signage on St. Charles Rd, and what do I need to submit? We'd like to have the new sign up before our grand reopening on {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi — for a replacement sign exceeding the existing size, you'll need a sign permit. Maximum wall sign area on St. Charles Rd commercial frontage is based on linear building frontage. Submit a drawing showing dimensions, materials, and mounting method. Fee is {AMOUNT}. Review takes {DAYS} business days.

body_tmpl: This is {FIRST} {LAST} from {BIZ}. We're taking over a vacant commercial space at {ADDRESS} and plan interior improvements: new partition walls, updated electrical, and ADA restroom. We have architectural drawings. Do we submit for all trades under one application? What's the plan review timeline and fee? We're trying to open by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Change of occupancy — new restaurant at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi — I'm opening a fast-casual restaurant at {ADDRESS}, formerly a retail space. I know a change-of-use inspection is required. We've upgraded the hood system and grease trap. What other inspections are needed before I can get a certificate of occupancy and open to the public? I've already submitted the health department application. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: For a restaurant conversion you'll need: building, electrical, plumbing, and fire inspections, plus Health Department sign-off. Once all inspections pass and we confirm fire marshal clearance, we issue the CO. Total timeline is typically {DAYS} business days after final inspection. Please submit updated floor plans if not already done.

body_tmpl: {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} here. Our contractor completed the grease trap installation last Friday. We need to schedule the plumbing inspection before we can receive our CO and open. Can you advise on the next available inspection date? We're available any morning after {TIME}. Permit case {CASE}.

Sandra — heads up that we have active building permits at {NUM} {STREET} (case {CASE}) and {NUM} {CROSS} (case {CASE}). Both projects involve exterior work. If your office gets any zoning or nuisance complaints on that block, please loop me in before issuing anything so we can confirm permit status. Thanks, Rosa.

Rosa — I reviewed the driveway expansion at {ADDRESS} (case {CASE}). The proposed impervious area increase of {NUM} sq ft triggers our stormwater ordinance threshold. Applicant will need to either reduce the footprint or show a gravel infiltration strip along the north edge. Let me know if you want me to reach out to them directly or if you'll handle it. — Tom
reply_tmpl: Tom — thanks. I'll add a correction comment in the system and send the applicant a deficiency letter. If they push back, feel free to call them directly. I'll cc you on the letter.

body_tmpl: David — attached is the permit fee summary for the period ending {DATE}. Total collected: {AMOUNT} across {NUM} permits. Notable: one commercial TI at {ADDRESS} accounted for {AMOUNT} of that. Let me know if finance needs any breakdowns by category for the quarterly report. — Rosa M.

subject_tmpl: Driveway permit — curb cut approval needed, {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Frank — the applicant at {ADDRESS} is requesting a new curb cut as part of a driveway permit (case {CASE}). Before I can issue, I need PW sign-off that the cut won't conflict with drainage infrastructure or existing utilities. Can you or your team do a quick field check? Target approval date is {DATE}. Thanks, Rosa.
reply_tmpl: Rosa — I'll send someone out by {DATE}. Preliminary look at GIS shows no conflicts but we'll confirm on the ground. I'll email you the field sign-off form same day.

body_tmpl: Rosa — received a FOIA request today for all permit records on {ADDRESS} from {DATE} to present. Case {CASE}. Can your office pull the records and send to me by {DATE}? Standard FOIA turnaround. Thanks, Yolanda.
reply_tmpl: Yolanda — I'll have my admin pull everything and send over by end of day {DATE}. Looks like there were {NUM} permits during that period. I'll flag any that are still open.

body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My backyard has significant erosion along the rear lot line and I want to build a retaining wall, about 40 feet long and {NUM} feet tall. My landscaper isn't sure if a permit is required. Is there a height threshold for Bellwood, and would an engineered design be required at that height?
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST} — retaining walls over four feet in height require a permit and a licensed engineer's stamped drawings. Under four feet, a permit is still recommended but no engineering letter is required. Fee is {AMOUNT}. Setback from property line must be at least equal to wall height. Happy to answer more questions.

body_tmpl: Hello — {NAME}, {ADDRESS}. Adding a gas line from the meter to a new outdoor kitchen area on my patio. Distance is about 30 feet. My plumber says he can do it but wants to know what the Village requires. Is a plumbing/gas permit required? Will an inspector need to be present for the pressure test, or is a final inspection sufficient?

subject_tmpl: Permit status — no update in {DAYS} days
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here — I submitted a building permit for a home addition at {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. It's been {DAYS} business days with no update and I'm concerned. Case number is {CASE}. My contractor is scheduled to mobilize in two weeks. Is there anything I can do to expedite, or is there information missing from my application?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — I checked case {CASE}. Your application is in plan review. The reviewer has one question about the foundation design that needs to be answered before we can approve. I'll send a formal deficiency notice today. Once you respond, we aim to close review within {DAYS} business days.

body_tmpl: Hi — I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. I pulled a deck permit (case {CASE}, fee paid {AMOUNT}) but I'm canceling the project due to budget reasons. No work was started. Can I request a refund of the permit fee, and if so, what portion is refundable? Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — if no inspections have been conducted and work has not started, you can request a refund of a portion of the fee. Our policy is to retain {AMOUNT} as an administrative fee and refund the remainder. Submit a written cancellation request referencing case {CASE} and we'll process it within {DAYS} business days.

body_tmpl: Hi, {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I want to convert my attached garage into a family room. I'd be adding insulation, drywall, a mini-split, and an electrical circuit. Do I need a building permit, electrical permit, or both? Will the square footage change affect my property taxes? I know that's maybe a county question but figured I'd ask.
reply_tmpl: Both a building and electrical permit are required for a garage conversion. The building permit covers the structural and thermal envelope changes; electrical covers the new circuit and mini-split wiring. As for taxes — you're right, that's Cook County Assessor's Office. Our office doesn't control that. Total estimated fees: {AMOUNT}.

body_tmpl: Just wanted to say thank you — our final inspection for the garage addition at {ADDRESS} passed this morning. The inspector was thorough and professional. It took a bit longer than expected but we understand you're busy. The whole permit process was smoother than I thought it would be. {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you so much, {FIRST} — that means a lot to hear! Congratulations on completing the project. Your CO will be ready within {DAYS} business days. Enjoy the new space!

body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My neighbor at {NUM} {STREET} has been constructing what looks like a large addition for three weeks. I've seen no permit posted on the property. Workers are there every day, including weekends, starting at 6 a.m. Can your office verify whether a permit was pulled and look into the early morning noise? Thank you.

Hello — I'm {NAME} and I'm helping my elderly mother at {ADDRESS} (age {AGE}) arrange for a wheelchair ramp to be built at her front entrance. The ramp will be about 12 feet long and made of aluminum. Is a permit required? Are there any fee waivers for accessibility modifications for senior residents? We're working with a nonprofit contractor.
reply_tmpl: An accessibility ramp permit is required, but the Village offers a reduced fee of {AMOUNT} for ADA-related residential modifications for residents age 65 and older. Please include documentation of the resident's age and a simple drawing of the ramp dimensions. We want to make this as smooth as possible.

subject_tmpl: Do I need a plumbing permit to move a sink?
body_tmpl: Hi, {FIRST} here at {ADDRESS}. I'm finishing my basement and want to move the utility sink about four feet to fit the layout better. It's the same drain stack, just extending the lines slightly. Does that require a plumbing permit? My contractor is saying yes; my brother-in-law says no. I'd rather hear it from the Village.
reply_tmpl: Your contractor is right — any extension or relocation of drain lines requires a plumbing permit in Bellwood. Fee is {AMOUNT}, and one inspection is required before the lines are covered. It's a quick process; your plumber can apply online.

body_tmpl: Good afternoon — {FIRST} {LAST} here. I had an inspection scheduled for {DATE} at {TIME} (case {CASE}, {ADDRESS}). Nobody showed up and I never got a call or email. I took time off work to be here. Can you tell me what happened and reschedule as soon as possible? My contractor is waiting to proceed.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST} — I sincerely apologize. There was a scheduling conflict on our end that was not communicated to you. I can offer you our next available slot on {DATE} at {TIME}, or we can prioritize you for an earlier opening if one comes up. I'm sorry for the inconvenience.

body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. I'm installing a Level 2 EV charger in my garage — 240V, 50-amp circuit. My electrician wants to know if Bellwood has a specific application for EV equipment or if it goes under a standard electrical permit. Also, is there a fast-track or same-day permit for simple electrical additions like this?
reply_tmpl: An EV charger installation is processed as a standard electrical permit — no special form needed. Fee is {AMOUNT}. We do offer over-the-counter approval for straightforward residential electrical work; bring a simple drawing showing the panel location, circuit length, and charger specs and we can often approve same day.
